adjudication_raw:
Review: Deep sequence model for cardiomyopathy variant pathogenicity.

Verdict: critique_wins

The 0.09 AUROC gap (0.93 vs. 0.84) is compromised by: (1) asymmetric threshold calibration between domain-specific model and generic PolyPhen-2 (AUROC is threshold-free but deployment claims are affected); (2) 40% benchmark concentration in two sarcomere hotspot genes with well-characterized pathogenic variants — classification is structurally easier there, and the overall AUROC may not generalize to the less-annotated portion of the panel; (3) PolyPhen-2 is the only comparator despite the paper claiming foundation-model representations outperform 'classical' predictors — modern deep-learning tools are absent. The clinical tool replacement recommendation is not supported by the evidence as presented.
